text: Recent research spanning five years (2009 – 2014) carried out in the northern part of Prague’s New Town has shed light on the medieval Bohemian era’s largest Jewish burial site, which lay there from the second half of the 13th century and up to the late 15th century. The research was carried out mainly in the Quadrio building site, an area heavily populated since the Early Middle Ages and that, owing to the relatively small area and continuity of the layout, remained virtually untouched until the construction of the new Prague metro lines in the 1970s. The new discoveries made regarding the location of the ancient Jewish cemetery make a further significant contribution to our knowledge of the complex character of the settlement structure of the historic area of Prague’s New Town, and also provide a further interesting chapter in the long history of the topography of the area.
explanation: Polished academic prose with credible, specific details; these could be AI-generated, but they also strongly suggest a human-authored source.
